_comment: Reproducibility manifest for the CUSTOM WOF gazetteer (admin-global-priority.db). We NEVER use the off-the-shelf geocode.earth prebuilt dumps (different WOF ids; deleted 2026-05-30). See the feedback-custom-wof-db-only memory + docs/articles/concepts/wof-data-pipeline.md. This file pins the exact build inputs so the artifact is reproducible — the gap that bit us (the admin-fr repo was deleted and never pinned, so the canonical 7-country DB couldn't be rebuilt) is closed by recording the commits below.
post_build: Three steps, IN ORDER (steps 1-2 were missing pre-2026-06-08; see docs/articles/evals/2026-06-08-honest-eval.md + #440). STEP 1: node --experimental-strip-types scripts/add-region-abbrevs.ts /mnt/playpen/mailwoman-data/wof/admin-global-priority.db   (enriches `names` with state/province abbreviations from chromium-i18n libaddressinput so findPlace('VT') resolves to Vermont; without it the region parent-constraint never engages and locality lookup runs unconstrained US-wide). STEP 2: node --experimental-strip-types scripts/backfill-ancestors-from-hierarchy.ts /mnt/playpen/mailwoman-data/wof/admin-global-priority.db   (repairs only-self ancestry for multi/ambiguous-parent places — e.g. NYC, parent_id=-4 — from wof:hierarchy, so the region-descendant boost can reach them; without it region resolution regresses metros like NYC -> 'New York Mills'). STEP 3: node resolver-wof-sqlite/out/build-fts-cli.js /mnt/playpen/mailwoman-data/wof/admin-global-priority.db   (builds place_search FTS5 INCLUDING the step-1 abbreviations + place_bbox R*Tree). Step 1 must precede step 3 (FTS reads `names`); step 2 is independent of FTS.

repos.name: whosonfirst-data-admin-nl
repos._added: 2026-06-04 for the coordinate-first postcode→locality work (NL coord-first now resolves)

expected_output._comment: Rebuilt 2026-06-04 to add ES/IT/NL (coordinate-first). DE/FR/GB locality counts unchanged (12657/57260/16813); DE PIP-containment held at 92.6%.

postcode_build._comment: US postcodes live in a SEPARATE shard DB (the resolver routes postalcode queries to it via multi-shard ATTACH). Built with the same build-unified-wof.ts using --placetypes postalcode.
postcode_build.post_build: Three steps, IN ORDER. STEP 1: node resolver-wof-sqlite/out/build-fts-cli.js /mnt/playpen/mailwoman-data/wof/postalcode-us.db   (FTS + R*Tree). STEP 2: node --experimental-strip-types scripts/fill-zcta-centroids.ts   (#525 pass 1: fills (0,0)-placeholder centroids from the Census ZCTA Gazetteer — /mnt/playpen/mailwoman-data/census/README.md — stamping per-row provenance in `centroid_source` as `census-zcta-2024`; never overwrites a real WOF coordinate; residual ~10% are PO-box/unique ZIPs with no ZCTA). STEP 3 (optional, requires US.txt): node --experimental-strip-types scripts/fill-zcta-centroids.ts --geonames /mnt/playpen/mailwoman-data/geonames/US.txt   (#525 pass 2: fills remaining (0,0) rows from GeoNames US postal file — download.geonames.org/export/zip/US.zip, CC-BY 4.0, attribute 'GeoNames (CC-BY 4.0)' in any artifact that ships these rows — stamping provenance as `geonames-us`; covers some PO-box/unique ZIPs not in ZCTA; idempotent, re-checks latitude=0). US.txt absent = step silently skipped (ZCTA residuals stay placeholder).
postcode_build.resolver_wiring: ResolveRouter + WofSqlitePlaceLookup take both DBs (admin-global-priority.db, postalcode-us.db); postalcode queries route to the postcode shard.

postcode_build_gb._comment: GB postcodes for the coordinate-first postcode→locality table (acquired from source 2026-06-04). 2.72M unit postcodes; geojson is committed directly (only meta CSVs are LFS).

postcode_locality_asset._comment: Coordinate-first candidate table (postcode → containing + nearby WOF localities). Read-only distributable asset; see resolver-wof-sqlite/POSTCODE-LOCALITY.md.

notes: admin-fr was missing from the cloned repos when this manifest was created (only the built admin-fr.db artifact remained); re-cloned 2026-05-30 at the commit above.
Clones are shallow (git clone --depth 1) for the data; the commit hash still pins the snapshot.
Postcodes: US (admin shard) + GB (postcode-build-gb) + DE/ES/FR/IT/NL (postalcode-intl.db) for the coordinate-first table.

2026-06-04: rebuilt admin-global-priority.db to add ES/IT/NL localities (NL coord-first now resolves). DE/FR/GB unchanged + DE PIP-containment held at 92.6% (no regression). Prior DB backed up as admin-global-priority.db.pre-nl-bak.

postcode_locality_cjk._comment: CJK postcode→locality via authoritative NAME-MATCH (not PIP — WOF CJK has no municipality polygons). Same postcode_locality table, consumed by the same postcode_area_resolution strategy. See resolver-wof-sqlite/POSTCODE-LOCALITY.md (CJK section) + docs/articles/evals/2026-06-05-cjk-arena.md.
postcode_locality_cjk.build: scripts/build-postcode-locality-cjk.py --country JP --postal-names KEN_ALL_ROME.CSV --geonames geonames/JP.txt --admin-db admin-global-priority.db --output postcode-locality-jp.db ; then merge into postcode-locality-intl.db

postal_authority.JP: KEN_ALL_ROME.zip (Japan Post, romanized) — CP932/Shift-JIS. Japan Post BLOCKS programmatic download (JS/geo-gated); fetch manually via browser: download.html → /zipcode/dl/roman-zip.html → roman/KEN_ALL_ROME.zip
postal_authority.KR: deferred — Juso.go.kr / Korea Post (login/geo-walled); GeoNames KR is Hangul vs WOF romaji
postal_authority.TW: deferred — no GeoNames postal; TW not yet in admin DB (admin-tw clone present)

postcode_locality_cjk.points_source: GeoNames postal (geonames/JP.txt, geonames/KR.txt) — already an in-project source for DE/ES/IT/NL
